title: Supply Chain, Browser Binaries, Packages, Containers, and Rollout · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in source-bound least-authority crawler release and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership.
Bind Playwright package, browser revision, download source, digest, platform libraries, sandbox policy, and patch process.
Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Dependency Provenance
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 5560 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Progressive Rollout
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-progressive-rollout-ljp882" that uses case 3113 to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Progressive Rollout.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Progressive Rollout into observable performance evidence.
